77 new episodes of DBZ airing every weekday at 5pm on Cartoon Network (began September 4th).



New DBZ on YTV Mondays from 8 PM to 9 PM.



Viz has begun censoring the DB manga. Our petition requesting unedited version can be found here.



FUNimation is looking for developers to make a DBZ game; however, nothing has been finalized yet



(Last Update: 09/30/2000)



DVD (FUNimation):
Trunks: Mysterious Youth
(Released)

(Released)

(9/19/2000)

(10/24/2000)

(11/21/2000)

(11/21/2000)

(October 2000)

Current Features: Full Episodes

We are in the middle of a server move, so there might be some unexpected down-time. Please bear with us.

Thursday - 10/05/2000

Mr.E @ 22:29 pst - Cartoon Network Ratings Surge...
Here's an interesting factoid:

Original animated programming seems to be working at Cartoon Network, as the cable channel posted a 25% prime-time ratings surge during this year´s third quarter, the Hollywood Reporter indicated. Cartoon Network pulled in an average of 1.26 million households, compared to a little more than 1 million during last year's comparable period. Powerpuff Girls and Dexter's Laboratory led the charge.

What's even more amazing is that anime did not pull the most people...two in-house CN shows did. I miss the days when they only showed Popeye, some old school WB cartoons, and the campy 60s stuff. Nostalgia is very amusing...their clothes are different from my clothes...ha ha ha. Story courtesy of AniMag.

SkullMac @ 21:28 pst - Server Status Updates
To answer the array of questions which have flooded our inboxes over the past week, here is the latest on our server move. Our new server is up and running. It will be accessible to everyone in a few short days (just as soon as our new NS records are created and our domain transfers). You'll know the new server when you hit it. Its much more responsive then our current server, it has a working FTP (!!!), and the title of our site will read: "Planet Namek - Now running on Linux Madness!"

There are a few more details I should probably fill you in on. First, and most importantly, is the new FTP server. The FTP server will house all of our downloadable files (movie clips, MP3s, manga translations, etc). We'll start the FTP out with a 100 user limit (each user will be limited to 30K/sec), and will tighten or loosen these restrictions as we see fit. (We don't want to end up with a $10,000 bandwidth bill for this month... and I'm not kidding here folks, bandwidth is EXPENSIVE!) In addition, everybody will be limited to one FTP connection at a time (i.e. you can't download more than one movie clip at a time). This shouldn't be too much of an inconvenience as 30K/sec is adequate to allow broadband users to download a file in no time flat and downloading two files at once would kill most dialup connections.

Now then, on to the message board. As many of you know, all old member account and posts were lost when GameFAN sold off some of its servers at the end of September. Yes, I know... this is a HUGE inconvenience. For the love of Kami, I CANNOT resurrect any accounts, post counts, messages, or ANYTHING from our old message board. Its ALL gone. With that aside, we will be using Ultra Board 2000 this time around, and with good reason. UB2k is far faster than our old message board, comes standard with many more features, and is extremely expandable. The new board will be available as soon as our domains transfer.

One last note. I will post 12 new movie clips when our domain transfers to the new server. These clips have been lovingly captured from the Ginyu: Assault subtitled DVD and encoded in two sizes: 56k and Cable.

Mr.E @ 00:13 pst - The Moment of Zen...
There's only one word that can sum up today's android saga update. Poop. I mean, excellence...yeah, excellence will do.

Wednesday - 10/04/2000

Mr.E @ 19:20 pst - "Interview" With Gen
This is more of a Q & A than an interview...but you can now read Gen's answers to some commonly asked questions on the official DBZ site. Gen addresses the full episode and fansub dilemma, theatrical movie releases, a DBZ video game, and the Freddie Prinze Jr story. Nothing really new is mentioned, though Gen does say the theatrical release is likely to be a double-feature of movies 6 and 7 (Which makes ABSOLUTELY NO SENSE. 5 and 6 are complementary movies, 6 and 7 have nothing to do with each other). Go check it out...you can find the link to the interview on their main news page.

Mr.E @ 00:51 pst - The Daily Update...
Blasted time zones...viva Swatch and GMT. It's only 11pm by me so uh...please don't hurt me. Here's your daily dose of the Android Saga.

Note: Thank you Tokyo-pop for the site review. :)

Mr.E @ 00:40 pst - 12" Figure Availability...
Three cheers for the Toy Island, who have really become a reliable source regarding DB-related play-things. They posted some info today regarding the 12" figures (with clothing...ooooo), so go check it out if playing with dolls is your bag (No offense intended towards anyone...well maybe those freaks stalking me).

Tuesday - 10/03/2000

Mr.E @ 09:24 pst - Hmmm...
DBZ has dropped a spot to #3 thanks to Halloween on the Lycos Top 50. I seem to recall the same thing happening last year...oh well, the holidays always climb to the top of the list; DBZ certainly isn't fading.

Mr.E @ 09:17 pst - No Delay in Bardock Special...
Got the reply from my source...

...the release date is still the same.

So there you have it...while the date could change in the coming weeks, there is no delay at the moment. As for when we'll see the DVD version...who knows...

Eric Phan @ 08:23 pst - Review of Dragon Ball Z Graphic Novel #1
Julian Grybowski, author of the petition for unedited Dragon Ball manga (which now has over 9,000 signatures - please sign it if you haven't already), has purchased Dragon Ball Z Graphic Novel #1 and shares his thoughts with us:

"I know that I may seem to be going back on my words about holding out until the uncensored ones (if at all) made their debut. But this one is so damn near uncensored it doesn't really matter. Don't get me wrong -- I'm still for unedited manga, but this has only two line changes, and they're fairly minor ones, at that.

The cover is indeed the same as the cover for Dragon Ball volume 17. This one has a blue motif for the lettering and background. I noticed a few things about the inside, as well. First of all, the cast of characters at the beginning was kept. I personally did not think it would make it because it shows all characters (except for Gohan and Raditz, of course) as they are during Goku's fight with Piccolo. This was seemingly made possible by the fact that Yamcha and Tenshinhan were eliminated from the image, because they are only in the first few chapters, to be included in Dragon Ball #16, and the Story So Far has been erased, because this is the start of a new arc in this arrangement. The table of contents still has Roshi carving on a stone, but the boxed title above it now reads "Dragon Ball Z" instead of "Dragon Ball 17" and the same title he had carved on the stone has been extended so only "Dragon Ball" is visible, and the gibberish (MOKUJ in big, bold letters) below it has been erased.

As for content itself, the first two chapters of DB 17 are indeed nonexistent, and in place of the cover image with all of the people at the Budokai, they use DBZ chapter 1's title image. for chapter 2, the line, "I'd've been perfectly happy fondling your breasts" has been replaced with "I'd've been perfectly happy with a nice big kiss". Bulma's next line has h ad the word "perverted" replaced with "ornery". All swearing has been left intact as well, despite my fears it wouldn't. Besides that, Viz replaced the cute lil' "Hi! I'm Son Gohan" image with chapter 3's title page!! Why? The title page gallery is missing a few pages as well, but I am assuming it will be in DBZ volume 2, from what my source has told me.

All in all, this was good, though I guess it lost a few points in my mind due to the fact of the chapters being off and whatnot, and I guess I'll feel the same way about DBZ volume 2 and Dragon Ball volume 16. Besides that, the relative lack of censorship was very pleasing, and was enough to get me to buy it. However, I am still completely for uncensored graphic novels, though my source still tells me that they're discussing it with their various licensors. When will they just get it over with?"

Mr.E @ 01:34 pst - Paragraph Up...
As promised, the next paragraph has been added to the android saga. And GameFan smells like old cheese...I had meant to make this update late Monday, but the server is now on Central time. Oh well, only a few more days...

Monday - 10/02/2000

Mr.E @ 23:36 pst - Random Notes...
Few things of interest today:

Firstly, I have purchased the new server that Namek will be moving to this week. It should be really fast, and it will have database support (yay!) I will be capping the FTP to 10 or 15k a sec...not because I like to be mean to broadband users, but because out of the 2000 dollars a month I would be paying, 1700 of that would go to bandwidth if I left it unregulated. That's not a small lump of change. However, we'll be adding some new low bandwidth clips for those who don't like waiting...and the rest of the site will not be capped.

The toy island has been receiving word of where to find the series 14/15 figures...

From many of the e-mails I've been getting, it would appear that the new Irwin figures are starting to pop up all over in Target stores, as well as the Warner Brothers Studio store. Check for them there.

...and the DVDs are now hitting stores en masse. I've gotten numerous reports that there are some rather odd sub-titling errors...like a single word appearing at the top of the screen while the rest pops up on the bottom. The language settings also like to reset themselves mid-way through the discs, forcing you to go through the main menu. These are all errors that should have been caught when they were error-checking the discs, but they are quite easy to ignore/get around. They are better than the Ginyu discs, but there's still room to improve.

Expect an episode guide update here in an hour or two. Have fun everyone!

Sunday - 10/01/2000

Mr.E @ 16:25 pst - Amazon DVD Delay...
Express has yet to ship my discs, and it looks like Amazon.com is having the same problems. Here's their e-mail on why they are unable to fulfill the orders:

Thank you for writing to Amazon.com. I'm sorry about the delay in shipping your order to you.

We have ordered copies of Dragonball Z, Vol. 31 - Trunks: Mysterious Youth from our supplier, but unfortunately we have not yet received them. We do expect to receive a shipment within the next 2-3 days and as soon as we do, we will ship your order to you.

On the date of shipment, we will send you an e-mail message confirming the date, contents, and method of your shipment.

Please let us know if you have any further questions, and thanks for shopping at Amazon.com

So far no DVDs have yet to be released on schedule...let's hope FUNimation reverses this trend soon. It's unlikely you'll find the DVD in mass quantities anywhere except a few local shops. Thanks to Scott Brown for forwarding us the reply he got from Amazon!

Update: It seems only the online distros are experiencing the delays in shipping...Suncoasts everywhere are getting the discs in the dozens. We'll have a full report on the discs once we get our copies.

Mr.E @ 11:25 pst - Android Saga Updated...
I apologize for missing the last two days, the server has been consuming most of my time recently. However, I have made it up to you, and there are now three more paragraphs in the android saga. Amazing how compact I could make the Furiza saga simply because of the amount of flashbacks and repetitve battles between Goku and Furiza. It's really difficult to do that with the androids :P

Saturday - 09/30/2000

Mr.E @ 23:03 pst - Title Fun...
Yeah, the old one was getting stale...so here's a brand new title image for all you boys and girls. I take full credit for this new title (although Skull helped a *little* with importing the scan, applying the filters, and figuring out the CGI). And what's that on the left side? Why, I do believe it's the contest semi-finalists!

Mr.E @ 22:23 pst - Message Board, Chat, MP3s, and Movie Clips...
...are all down. :( The joys of moving to a new host...I should have my server set up sometime next week, in which case all those sections will return one by one. Apologies.

Mr.E @ 14:41 pst - Bardock Special Delayed?
Here's the story from AnimeNation.com:

Funimation has placed the VHS release of the DBZ: Bardock TV special on hold until further notice. The tape was originally scheduled for release next month.

And yes, that's all they posted on the subject. No sources, no links, nothing. It's very possible the special was delayed, but I would take this with a island-sized grain of salt. I am getting confirmation as we speak...thanks to KiGlow for alerting us...

Mr.E @ 14:34 pst - Irwin Toy Explanation...
I posted this on Thursday, but it got lost in the server problems...here's why you haven't seen series 14/15 figures at dbzstore.com:

Basically, they said that Irwin was going to ship the "specialty" packaging to the funimation store (The packaging that is supposed to be shipped to the specialty markets such as comic stores)... What Irwin failed to tell funimation was that the specialty packaging would not be available until the end of October. Upon finding this out, DBZSTORE.COM changed their order to normal packaging to get the figures up on the site quicker. This was the last time they heard from Irwin... Irwin isn't returning Funimations calls, and they've been expecting the figures for two weeks now. Due to Irwins silence, Funimation is being left completely in the dark as to why they haven't seen the figures. I was also informed that the "delay" in Series 14 and 15 to commercial stores such as Walmart, etc, isn't because of the change of packaging... (As reported by EX)... They misunderstood... the delay is the one named above... and it's to DBZSTORE.COM and not to commercial markets... (I was told this was partly in fault of the person who gave EX the information, as they weren't exactly clear)

But....
The good news:
"When we do get the toys in stock I will have them on the page a.s.a.p., probably within 2 hours of the truck arriving at our dock."

So, get em at amazon.com and local dealers for now. They are really nice collectables. Courtesy of the Dragonball Toy Island.

Mr.E @ 14:30 pst - Well That Sucked...
Seems GameFan had moved us to another server without telling us, and we reverted a few days (Yes, I did update Thursday). Sorry about that everyone, it's fixed now. I have a good host in mind...and I already have a new advertiser (as you can see above), so we should manage. I'll have two paragraphs for you in the episode guide today to make up for missing yesterday's.

Wednesday - 09/27/2000

Mr.E @ 23:46 pst - Android Saga...
Today's paragraph is up. Yep.

Disclaimers
Planet Namek is just a fansite, we did NOT create Dragonball, nor did we have any part in the production process.

©2000, Bird Studio/Shueisha, Toei Animation. Licensed by FUNimation Productions, Inc. All Rights Reserved, or as otherwise directed by FUNimation. This site is not meant to infringe on any copyrights, it is simply here to encourage people to watch the Dragonball series.

Although we did not create the series, we did take a lot of time to work on this site and bring you original files and information. Please honor our efforts by not stealing our files/layout from us. Thank you.

Web Page Design © Planet Namek

Best viewed in 800 by 600 resolution or better & thousands of colors or more.
For the best experience, set your browser to view images "better" rather than "faster."
